items.question: Cross-validation for overfitting mitigation primarily helps by:
options: Averaging model comparisons across folds instead of one validation slice
Eliminating the need for any regularization
Training on the test set multiple times
Replacing all hold-out splits with LOOCV always
items.answer: Averaging model comparisons across folds instead of one validation slice
items.explanation: CV reduces overfitting to partition luck during selection; it does not remove model capacity limits.

items.question: After GridSearchCV picks hyperparameters on train+val, you should:
options: Refit on all development data before one locked test evaluation
Report best_score_ as the final unbiased test metric
Skip refit and deploy the last fold's partial train model
Re-grid-search on the test set if test score is lower
items.answer: Refit on all development data before one locked test evaluation
items.explanation: CV selects settings; refit uses all dev labels; test is touched once.

items.question: Nested cross-validation addresses:
options: Tuning optimism when many settings compete on inner folds
Missing values in the feature matrix
Class imbalance without stratification
GPU memory limits during training
items.answer: Tuning optimism when many settings compete on inner folds
items.explanation: Outer folds score inner winners on data not used for that round's selection.

items.question: Two models have mean CV AUC 0.84 vs 0.845 but std 0.01 vs 0.06. Mitigation-minded choice:
options: Prefer the model with lower std if means are close
Always pick the highest mean regardless of std
Pick the highest std model for exploration
Ignore both and tune on the test set
items.answer: Prefer the model with lower std if means are close
items.explanation: Low fold spread suggests less overfit-prone behavior across partitions.

items.question: k-fold fold construction and stratification rules are canonical in:
options: Model Evaluation → Cross-Validation
This overfitting mitigation topic only
Train/Val/Test Splits exclusively
Random Forests hyperparameter docs only
items.explanation: This topic applies CV machinery to selection and tuning; protocol details live under Model Evaluation.
